task_completion.description: Create the global 'Color' attribute with all specified terms
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WooCommerce 'Attributes' page in the WordPress admin. Verify that an attribute named 'Color' exists and its 'Terms' column explicitly lists: Black, White, Blue, Red, Green, and Grey.

task_completion.description: Create the global 'Material' attribute with all specified terms
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WooCommerce 'Attributes' page in the WordPress admin. Verify that an attribute named 'Material' exists and its 'Terms' column explicitly lists: Cotton, Polyester, Denim, Wool, and Leather.

task_completion.description: Assign Color and Material to the 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' and make them visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Product Edit page for 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' (Product Data -> Attributes tab) or its frontend product page. Verify 'Color' is set to 'Black' and 'Material' is set to 'Cotton'. If in the backend, ensure the 'Visible on the product page' checkbox is checked for both.

task_completion.description: Assign Color and Material to the 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' and make them visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Product Edit page for 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' (Product Data -> Attributes tab) or its frontend product page. Verify 'Color' is set to 'Blue' and 'Material' is set to 'Denim'. If in the backend, ensure the 'Visible on the product page' checkbox is checked for both.

task_completion.description: Assign Color and Material to the 'Merino Wool Sweater' and make them visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Product Edit page for 'Merino Wool Sweater' (Product Data -> Attributes tab) or its frontend product page. Verify 'Color' is set to 'Grey' and 'Material' is set to 'Wool'. If in the backend, ensure the 'Visible on the product page' checkbox is checked for both.

integrity.description: Agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin) or frontend store, not a terminal running WP-CLI commands or direct database queries.

integrity.description: Agent actually interacted with the application forms
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of typing in text fields, clicking 'Add new attribute', selecting terms from dropdowns, and clicking 'Save attributes' or 'Update' buttons.

integrity.description: Results are genuinely saved in the system, not spoofed via browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should look like standard WooCommerce without open browser developer tools or console scripts manipulating the DOM to fake the presence of attributes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Task requires creating two global attributes: 'Color' (slug: color, terms: Black, White, Blue, Red, Green, Grey) and 'Material' (slug: material, terms: Cotton, Polyester, Denim, Wool, Leather). Three products must have these assigned and set to visible on the product page: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' (SKU: OCT-BLK-M) needs Color=Black, Material=Cotton. 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' (SKU: SFDJ-BLU-32) needs Color=Blue, Material=Denim. 'Merino Wool Sweater' (SKU: MWS-GRY-L) needs Color=Grey, Material=Wool.
